prerequisites: A UserVoice account with admin access, since API keys are created from the admin console.
Your UserVoice account subdomain, the SUBDOMAIN in https://SUBDOMAIN.uservoice.com, which is required to build the API base URL.
A UserVoice Admin API key created under Settings, Integrations, UserVoice API keys.
A start date, used as the earliest point from which to sync incremental records.

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single UserVoice Admin API key, supplied in the api_key field. UserVoice sends it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <api_key>) against the Admin API at https://SUBDOMAIN.uservoice.com/api/v2/. Create the key in the admin console under Settings, Integrations, UserVoice API keys.

setupSteps.title: Create a UserVoice Admin API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your UserVoice admin console and go to Settings, Integrations, UserVoice API keys. Click Add API Key, enter a descriptive name, and leave the Application URL and Callback URL fields blank. Trusted clients have full admin-level access including the ability to delete content, so treat the resulting key as a sensitive secret. Click Add API key to generate it.

setupSteps.title: Find your account subdomain
setupSteps.body: Identify the subdomain portion of your UserVoice URL. For an account reached at https://acme.uservoice.com the subdomain is acme. The connector uses it to construct the API base URL https://SUBDOMAIN.uservoice.com/api/v2/, so provide it in the subdomain field.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated key in the api_key field, set subdomain to your account subdomain, and set start_date to the earliest date from which records should be synced. The connector then calls the UserVoice Admin API v2 using the key as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

sources.title: UserVoice API: Authenticating and Making your First Call (API key)
